nodes.x: The proverbs of Solomon the son of David, king of Israel;
These are also proverbs of Solomon, which the men of Hezekiah king of Judah copied out.

Solomon, the son of David, was endowed with wisdom above the sons of men, and chosen of the Lord to rear Him a Temple, the like of which the world has not seen before or since ; and was otherwise blessed and prospered above all the kings of Israel. This man was likewise a polygamist whilst the Lord favoured him so remarkably.
The proverbs of Solomon. A wise son maketh a glad father: but a foolish son is the heaviness of his mother.

And David said to Solomon his son, Be strong and of good courage, and do it: fear not, nor be dismayed: for the Lord God, even my God, will be with thee; he will not fail thee, nor forsake thee, until thou hast finished all the work for the service of the house of the Lord.
So when David was old and full of days, he made Solomon his son king over Israel.
To know wisdom and instruction; to perceive the words of understanding;
To receive the instruction of wisdom, justice, and judgment, and equity;
Wisdom is the principal thing; therefore get wisdom: and with all thy getting get understanding.
To another is given the word of knowledge, that all may be taught to be wise and to have knowledge.
For to one is given by the Spirit the word of wisdom; to another the word of knowledge by the same Spirit;
But where shall wisdom be found? and where is the place of understanding?
To receive the instruction of wisdom, justice, and judgment, and equity;
To know wisdom and instruction; to perceive the words of understanding;
Justice and judgment are the habitation of thy throne: mercy and truth shall go before thy face.
Buy the truth, and sell it not; also wisdom, and instruction, and understanding.
To give subtilty to the simple, to the young man knowledge and discretion.
And beheld among the simple ones, I discerned among the youths, a young man void of understanding,
A wise man will hear, and will increase learning; and a man of understanding shall attain unto wise counsels:
Give instruction to a wise man, and he will be yet wiser: teach a just man, and he will increase in learning.
A wise man is strong; yea, a man of knowledge increaseth strength.
Now, behold this is wisdom; whoso readeth, let him understand and receive also;
Hear my words, O ye wise men; and give ear unto me, ye that have knowledge.
To understand a proverb, and the interpretation; the words of the wise, and their dark sayings.
And moreover, because the preacher was wise, he still taught the people knowledge; yea, he gave good heed, and sought out, and set in order many proverbs.
So that thou incline thine ear unto wisdom, and apply thine heart to understanding;
Who is as the wise man? and who knoweth the interpretation of a thing? a man’s wisdom maketh his face to shine, and the boldness of his face shall be changed.
Wisdom is before him that hath understanding; but the eyes of a fool are in the ends of the earth.
¶ The fear of the Lord is the beginning of knowledge: but fools despise wisdom and instruction.
The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom: and the knowledge of the holy is understanding.
The fear of the Lord is the beginning of wisdom: a good understanding have all they that do his commandments: his praise endureth for ever.
The fear of the Lord is the instruction of wisdom; and before honour is humility.
For that they hated knowledge, and did not choose the fear of the Lord:
And unto man he said, Behold, the fear of the Lord, that is wisdom; and to depart from evil is understanding.
My son, hear the instruction of thy father, and forsake not the law of thy mother:
¶ My son, keep thy father’s commandment, and forsake not the law of thy mother:
My son, forget not my law; but let thine heart keep my commandments:
And his mother said unto him, Upon me be thy curse, my son: only obey my voice, and go fetch me them.
My son, if thou wilt receive my words, and hide my commandments with thee;
For they shall be an ornament of grace unto thy head, and chains about thy neck.
So shall they be life unto thy soul, and grace to thy neck.

And their lips shall extol the name of the Lord of Spirits,
    And righteousness before Him shall never fail,
    [And uprightness shall never fail before Him].

For Thy grace hath been great towards me, And great hath been Thy mercy to my soul; Let Thy grace be lift up upon my sons, And let not wicked spirits rule over them Lest they should destroy them from the earth.
And they shall be upon thee for a sign and for a wonder, and upon thy seed for ever.

For they shall not lead away captive the daughters of my people because of their tenderness, save I shall visit them with a sore curse, even unto destruction; for they shall not commit whoredoms, like unto them of old, saith the Lord of Hosts.
¶ My son, if sinners entice thee, consent thou not.
¶ Now, lo, if he beget a son, that seeth all his father’s sins which he hath done, and considereth, and doeth not such like,
Against thee, thee only, have I sinned, and done this evil in thy sight: that thou mightest be justified when thou speakest, and be clear when thou judgest.
Yet thou sayest, Because I am innocent, surely his anger shall turn from me. Behold, I will plead with thee, because thou sayest, I have not sinned.
If they say, Come with us, let us lay wait for blood, let us lurk privily for the innocent without cause:
And they lay wait for their own blood; they lurk privily for their own lives.
So shalt thou put away the guilt of innocent blood from among you, when thou shalt do that which is right in the sight of the Lord.
The words of the wicked are to lie in wait for blood: but the mouth of the upright shall deliver them.

Bishop Coxe, of the Protestant Episcopal Church of New York, in a pastoral letter to his people, writes: “I have heretofore warned my flock against the blood guiltiness of antenatal infanticide. If any doubts existed heretofore as to the propriety of my warnings on this subject, they must now disappear before the fact that the world itself is beginning to be horrified by the practical results of…

will come, as sure as we live, when distress and calamity will fall upon the wicked, and our own nation has a great deal to answer for. They have to answer for deeds that cannot be easily paid. The blood of innocence has stained the soil of free America—the blood of a Prophet, of a Patriarch, and of other righteous men and women who have suffered for their religion, and for no other cause than…
Let us swallow them up alive as the grave; and whole, as those that go down into the pit:
They shall go down to the bars of the pit, when our rest together is in the dust.
They shall bring thee down to the pit, and thou shalt die the deaths of them that are slain in the midst of the seas.
They, and all that appertained to them, went down alive into the pit, and the earth closed upon them: and they perished from among the congregation.
O Lord, thou hast brought up my soul from the grave: thou hast kept me alive, that I should not go down to the pit.

Yes, we will lay down these bodies in the grave. What for? That the
dust, our mother earth, that composes the house of the spirit, may
be purified by passing through this ordeal, and be prepared to be
called up and united with the intelligent heavenly body that God has
prepared. This is nothing but a change. It is not the dissolution of
the creature; it is merely putting off the flesh that…
We shall find all precious substance, we shall fill our houses with spoil:
Thy substance and thy treasures will I give to the spoil without price, and that for all thy sins, even in all thy borders.
And I will give it into the hands of the strangers for a prey, and to the wicked of the earth for a spoil; and they shall pollute it.

And they shall make a spoil of thy riches, and make a prey of thy merchandise: and they shall break down thy walls, and destroy thy pleasant houses: and they shall lay thy stones and thy timber and thy dust in the midst of the water.

to bless the soil, the atmosphere and the water. Then we have our crops, our fruit, our flocks and herds to live upon, to improve upon, and then go on and make our clothing, build houses, improve our streets, our cities and all our surroundings and make them beautiful; beautify every place with the workmanship of our own hands. Keep what is necessary, dispose of what we may have to dispose of. To…
Cast in thy lot among us; let us all have one purse:

And it came to pass, when we came to the inn, that we opened our sacks, and, behold, every man’s money was in the mouth of his sack, our money in full weight: and we have brought it again in our hand.
And let my servant Oliver Cowdery have the lot which is set off joining the house, which is to be for the printing office, which is lot number one, and also the lot upon which his father resides.

purse, which I will give you.' He brought it to me, and I thanked him for it. We went a few rods farther and met Brother Heber C. Kimball, in the same condition, also hewing a log to build a cabin. He said : 'As Parley has given you a purse, I have got a dollar I will give you to put in it.' He gave me both a dollar and a blessing.
And he hath cast the lot for them, and his hand hath divided it unto them by line: they shall possess it for ever, from generation to generation shall they dwell therein.
Then said he unto them, But now, he that hath a purse, let him take it, and likewise his scrip: and he that hath no sword, let him sell his garment, and buy one.
My son, walk not thou in the way with them; refrain thy foot from their path:
Then shalt thou walk in thy way safely, and thy foot shall not stumble.

O Lord, wilt thou encircle me around in the robe of thy righteousness! O Lord, wilt thou make a way for mine escape before mine enemies! Wilt thou make my path straight before me! Wilt thou not place a stumbling block in my way—but that thou wouldst clear my way before me, and hedge not up my way, but the ways of mine enemy.
Ponder the path of thy feet, and let all thy ways be established.
Withdraw thy foot from thy neighbour’s house; lest he be weary of thee, and so hate thee.

And saith unto him, If thou be the Son of God, cast thyself down: for it is written, He shall give his angels charge concerning thee: and in their hands they shall bear thee up, lest at any time thou dash thy foot against a stone.
For their feet run to evil, and make haste to shed blood.
Their feet run to evil, and they make haste to shed innocent blood: their thoughts are thoughts of iniquity; wasting and destruction are in their paths.
Their feet are swift to shed blood:
To deliver their soul from death, and to keep them alive in famine.
And they shall take of the blood, and strike it on the two side posts and on the upper door post of the houses, wherein they shall eat it.
Surely in vain the net is spread in the sight of any bird.
Thus saith the Lord God; I will therefore spread out my net over thee with a company of many people; and they shall bring thee up in my net.
And thou shalt put it under the compass of the altar beneath, that the net may be even to the midst of the altar.

¶ If a bird’s nest chance to be before thee in the way in any tree, or on the ground, whether they be young ones, or eggs, and the dam sitting upon the young, or upon the eggs, thou shalt not take the dam with the young:
There is a path which no fowl knoweth, and which the vulture’s eye hath not seen:
And they lay wait for their own blood; they lurk privily for their own lives.
For their feet run to evil, and make haste to shed blood.
If they say, Come with us, let us lay wait for blood, let us lurk privily for the innocent without cause:
Behold, here is wisdom. Let them do this lest they receive none inheritance, save it be by the shedding of blood.

That their souls may escape the wrath of God, the desolation of abomination which awaits the wicked, both in this world and in the world to come. Verily, I say unto you, let those who are not the first elders continue in the vineyard until the mouth of the Lord shall call them, for their time is not yet come; their garments are not clean from the blood of this generation.
So are the ways of every one that is greedy of gain; which taketh away the life of the owners thereof.
To some is given one, and to some is given another, that all may be profited thereby.
But the wise answered, saying, Not so; lest there be not enough for us and you: but go ye rather to them that sell, and buy for yourselves.
For what shall it profit a man, if he shall gain the whole world, and lose his own soul?
¶ Wisdom crieth without; she uttereth her voice in the streets:
She crieth in the chief place of concourse, in the openings of the gates: in the city she uttereth her words, saying,
She crieth at the gates, at the entry of the city, at the coming in at the doors.
¶ The voice of him that crieth in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make straight in the desert a highway for our God.
Now is she without, now in the streets, and lieth in wait at every corner.)
She crieth in the chief place of concourse, in the openings of the gates: in the city she uttereth her words, saying,
¶ Wisdom crieth without; she uttereth her voice in the streets:
She hath sent forth her maidens: she crieth upon the highest places of the city,

This is the rejoicing city that dwelt carelessly, that said in her heart, I am, and there is none beside me: how is she become a desolation, a place for beasts to lie down in! every one that passeth by her shall hiss, and wag his hand.
32 Moreover, they who were in the city said there was no harlot in the gate, because she came from another place, and sat for a while in the gate.
How long, ye simple ones, will ye love simplicity? and the scorners delight in their scorning, and fools hate knowledge?
Smite a scorner, and the simple will beware: and reprove one that hath understanding, and he will understand knowledge.
A prudent man concealeth knowledge: but the heart of fools proclaimeth foolishness.
A scorner seeketh wisdom, and findeth it not: but knowledge is easy unto him that understandeth.
¶ The fear of the Lord is the beginning of knowledge: but fools despise wisdom and instruction.
Understanding is a wellspring of life unto him that hath it: but the instruction of fools is folly.
Turn you at my reproof: behold, I will pour out my spirit unto you, I will make known my words unto you.
And it shall be given unto you, power over that spirit; and you shall proclaim against that spirit with a loud voice that it is not of God—
And ye shall go forth in the power of my Spirit, preaching my gospel, two by two, in my name, lifting up your voices as with the sound of a trump, declaring my word like unto angels of God.

Verily, verily I say unto you, 1 who speak even by the voice of my spirit: eyen Alpha and Omega, your Lord and your God; hearken, O ye who have given your names to go forth to prench my gospel, and to prune my vineyard; Behold I say unto you, that it is my will that you should go forth and not tarry, neither be idle, but labor with your mights; lifting up your voices as with the sound of a trump,…

And now my beloved brethren, I have spoken these words unto you according to the Spirit which testifieth in me; and my soul doth exceedingly rejoice, because of the exceeding diligence and heed which ye have given unto my word.
¶ Because I have called, and ye refused; I have stretched out my hand, and no man regarded;

Wherefore, when I came, was there no man? when I called, was there none to answer? Is my hand shortened at all, that it cannot redeem? or have I no power to deliver? behold, at my rebuke I dry up the sea, I make the rivers a wilderness: their fish stinketh, because there is no water, and dieth for thirst.
Thine hands have made me and fashioned me together round about; yet thou dost destroy me.
When I called again there was none of you to answer; yet my arm was not shortened at all that I could not redeem, neither my power to deliver.

Wherefore, when I came, there was no man; when I called, yea, there was none to answer. O house of Israel, is my hand shortened at all that it cannot redeem, or have I no power to deliver? Behold, at my rebuke I dry up the sea, I make their rivers a wilderness and their fish to stink because the waters are dried up, and they die because of thirst.
And now this calling and commandment give I unto you concerning all men—
But ye have set at nought all my counsel, and would none of my reproof:
They would none of my counsel: they despised all my reproof.
Counsel is mine, and sound wisdom: I am understanding; I have strength.
For I have not shunned to declare unto you all the counsel of God.
Take counsel together, and it shall come to naught; speak the word, and it shall not stand; for God is with us.
Have not I written to thee excellent things in counsels and knowledge,
I also will laugh at your calamity; I will mock when your fear cometh;
When your fear cometh as desolation, and your destruction cometh as a whirlwind; when distress and anguish cometh upon you.
Be not afraid of sudden fear, neither of the desolation of the wicked, when it cometh.
The Lord shall laugh at him: for he seeth that his day is coming.
The righteous also shall see, and fear, and shall laugh at him:
When your fear cometh as desolation, and your destruction cometh as a whirlwind; when distress and anguish cometh upon you.
Fearfulness and trembling are come upon me, and horror hath overwhelmed me.
I also will laugh at your calamity; I will mock when your fear cometh;

And it shall come to pass, that he who fleeth from the noise of the fear shall fall into the pit; and he that cometh up out of the midst of the pit shall be taken in the snare: for the windows from on high are open, and the foundations of the earth do shake.
Then shall they call upon me, but I will not answer; they shall seek me early, but they shall not find me:
And they shall say to you, See here; or, see there: go not after them, nor follow them.
He that seeketh me early shall find me, and shall not be forsaken.
Ye shall seek me, and shall not find me: and where I am, thither ye cannot come.
As soon as they hear of me, they shall obey me: the strangers shall submit themselves unto me.
He shall call upon me, and I will answer him: I will be with him in trouble; I will deliver him, and honour him.
For that they hated knowledge, and did not choose the fear of the Lord:

For they knew that the prophets had testified of these things for many years, and that the sign which had been given was already at hand; and they began to fear because of their iniquity and their unbelief.

Therefore they did not fear Ammon, for they supposed that one of their men could slay him according to their pleasure, for they knew not that the Lord had promised Mosiah that he would deliver his sons out of their hands; neither did they know anything concerning the Lord; therefore they delighted in the destruction of their brethren; and for this cause they stood to scatter the flocks of the…
And even as they did not like to retain God in their knowledge, God gave them over to a reprobate mind, to do those things which are not convenient;
They would none of my counsel: they despised all my reproof.
But if they had stood in my counsel, and had caused my people to hear my words, then they should have turned them from their evil way, and from the evil of their doings.
But ye have set at nought all my counsel, and would none of my reproof:
But behold, their anger did increase against me, insomuch that they did seek to take away my life.
In the day of their peace they esteemed lightly my counsel; but, in the day of their trouble, of necessity they feel after me.
Yea, young children despised me; I arose, and they spake against me.
Therefore shall they eat of the fruit of their own way, and be filled with their own devices.
They shall still bring forth fruit in old age; they shall be fat and flourishing;
Behold, they have labored exceedingly, and have brought forth much fruit; and how great shall be their reward!
They shall build, and another shall not inherit it; they shall plant vineyards, and they shall eat the fruit thereof. Even so. Amen.
And the land shall yield her fruit, and ye shall eat your fill, and dwell therein in safety.
And they shall build houses, and inhabit them; and they shall plant vineyards, and eat the fruit of them.
For the turning away of the simple shall slay them, and the prosperity of fools shall destroy them.
Evil shall slay the wicked: and they that hate the righteous shall be desolate.
And the company shall stone them with stones, and dispatch them with their swords; they shall slay their sons and their daughters, and burn up their houses with fire.
I said unto the fools, Deal not foolishly: and to the wicked, Lift not up the horn:
And ye shall tread down the wicked; for they shall be ashes under the soles of your feet in the day that I shall do this, saith the Lord of hosts.
For ye suffer fools gladly, seeing ye yourselves are wise.
But whoso hearkeneth unto me shall dwell safely, and shall be quiet from fear of evil.
And those which remain shall hear, and fear, and shall henceforth commit no more any such evil among you.
¶ Therefore thus saith the Lord, Behold, I will bring evil upon them, which they shall not be able to escape; and though they shall cry unto me, I will not hearken unto them.
Verily, I say unto you, that woe shall come unto the inhabitants of the earth if they will not hearken unto my words;
And it shall come to pass, that whosoever will not hearken unto my words which he shall speak in my name, I will require it of him.
O hearken, ye elders of my church, and give ear to the words which I shall speak unto you.
